extraction.one_line_summary: ACKERMANN Pascal won the Stage 2 sprint from off the pace at 250m, overpowering Viviani and Gaviria who faded after leading out early.
tactical_patterns: Ackermann deliberately waited until 250m while rivals launched early, demonstrating superior acceleration over traditional long sprint leadouts.
Bora-Hansgrohe positioned McCarthy and Ackermann through technical corners without traditional leadout train, abandoning conventional sprint setup.
Multiple crashes in final 3.5km during positioning suggests technical corners trump pure power in Tuscan finales.

rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: positive: First Grand Tour stage win by consciously delaying sprint launch while Viviani/Gaviria exhausted themselves leading out
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: Led out too early and faded despite perfect local knowledge as Italian champion in Tuscany
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: negative: Launched sprint prematurely and couldn't sustain power to line despite appearing strongest at 300m
rider_signals.CHEKHONIN Ilya (CICCONE Giulio): positive: Extended King of Mountains lead by attacking solo on uncategorized Apennine climb, showing tactical awareness for jersey defense
